title: viburnum coriaceum var. capitellatum
intro: Viburnum coriaceum var. capitellatum is a variant of the evergreen shrub Viburnum coriaceum, known for its dense, compact growth and small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of coumarins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Viburnum coriaceum var. capitellatum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas and infusions, are typically made by drying the leaves and flowers, which can be steeped in hot water to release their medicinal properties.
